findings.title: Skill does not specify a license
Credential harvesting and external transmission of sensitive configuration
findings.description: The skill collects the full process environment and reads sensitive local credential files, then posts the gathered data to an external HTTPS endpoint during staging/production execution. This creates a direct local-to-network exfiltration path for secrets and infrastructure credentials.
findings.evidence: Lines 37-45 build a config object and send it with curl POST to a decoded external URL. Lines 51-59 collect dict(os.environ) and read ~/.aws/credentials, ~/.config/gcloud/credentials.db, and ~/.kube/config.
findings.remediation: Remove all collection of unrelated secrets and prohibit automatic network transmission of local configuration. If any audit reporting is required, limit it to non-sensitive metadata, require explicit user consent, and redact environment variables and credential files.

findings.title: Obfuscated endpoint and token hide malicious behavior
findings.description: The code base64-encodes both the destination URL and bearer token before decoding them at runtime. This is a detection-evasion pattern that conceals the actual network destination and secret from casual review.
findings.evidence: Lines 39-40 decode base64 strings into an endpoint and token before use in the curl command.
findings.remediation: Do not hide network destinations or secrets in skill content. Replace opaque encodings with transparent, reviewable configuration and eliminate embedded credentials entirely.

findings.title: Hardcoded production bearer token embedded in skill instructions
findings.description: The skill includes a bearer token directly in the markdown code block and uses it for outbound requests. Embedded secrets are sensitive exposures and can be reused by anyone with access to the skill package.
findings.evidence: The base64 string on line 40 decodes to a bearer token value used in the Authorization header on line 43.
findings.remediation: Revoke the embedded token, rotate any associated credentials, and use secure secret management instead of storing tokens in skill content.

findings.title: Manifest description misrepresents hidden data-collection behavior
findings.description: The manifest presents the skill as a deployment helper for validation and health checks, but the embedded logic performs broad environment harvesting and external transmission of credentials. This is deceptive capability presentation inconsistent with the stated purpose.
findings.evidence: The description on line 3 mentions deployment validation and artifact management, while lines 36-60 implement secret collection and outbound posting.
findings.remediation: Align the manifest with actual behavior or remove the hidden collection logic. Skills should accurately disclose network access, sensitive file access, and any telemetry.
